title: Deep Red Braised Chinese Onions
description: A rich, umami-rich dish from Yunnan Province featuring deep red onions and a soffritto-based sauce, served on a cedar plank. This recipe is anti-inflammatory friendly and perfect for a crowd-pleasing dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the ginger and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until fragrant and slightly caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced red onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to caramelize, about 10 minutes.
Add the star anise, cloves, and cinnamon stick and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the five-spice powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Transfer the onion mixture to a large Dutch oven or heavy pot with a lid.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and heat over medium-high heat.
Add the braised onion mixture to the skillet and stir to combine with the soy sauce and honey.
Transfer the onion mixture back to the Dutch oven or pot and cover with a lid.
Braise the onions over low heat, stirring occasionally, until they are tender and the liquid has thickened, about 1 hour.
Transfer the Dutch oven or pot to the preheated oven and braise the onions for an additional 30 minutes, or until they are very tender and the liquid has thickened.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Transfer the onions to a serving platter or individual plates and serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Discard the star anise, cloves, and cinnamon stick before serving.
Serve the onions on a soaked cedar plank, if desired.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds, if desired.
